rationale: Probes via rdm story mutation-probe on rdm/story_audit/mutation.py, all KILLED (module under review is the probe machinery itself: the running process restores with the imported original while the pytest subprocess re-imports the mutant). Original clauses (test_mutation_probe_applies_reports_and_restores): (1) applies the mutation live: writing the original instead of original.replace(find,replace,1) KILLED (stub runner asserts 'VALUE = 2' on disk during the run); (2) reports killed: inverting "killed": not tests_passed -> tests_passed KILLED; (3) reports survived: inverting "survived" KILLED; (4) always restores: replacing the finally-block '_write(file_path, original)  # always revert' with pass KILLED (file==original asserted after killed AND survived paths, plus res['restored']); (5) exactly-once application (ambiguous site rejected): occurrences != 1 -> occurrences < 1 KILLED ('error' in result + file untouched asserted). Defense clauses (test_mutation_probe_restore_survives_interruption): (6) journal-before-mutating: journaling '' instead of the original KILLED (runner reads the journal mid-window and asserts it equals the original), and leaving the journal behind (unlink -> exists) KILLED (not journal.exists() after a normal probe); (7) interrupted-probe recovery: neutering the restore write in recover_interrupted_probe KILLED (res['recovered'] and file==original and journal gone; recover_interrupted_probe(src) is False afterwards); (8) termination signal still restores: not installing the SIGTERM handler (signal.signal -> signal.getsignal) KILLED (pytest.raises(KeyboardInterrupt) + file restored + no journal; without the handler TERM kills the process instead); (9) per-write cache invalidation via unique mtimes: constant mtime os.utime ns=(1,1) KILLED and second-truncated mtime (int(time.time())*1e9, the exact pyc mtime-seconds granularity / same-second scenario) KILLED via len({before,during,after})==3. Equivalent mutant documented: deleting the _bump_mtime call from _write SURVIVED because this filesystem timestamps consecutive writes with naturally distinct nanoseconds - the assertion pins the required observable (three distinct mtimes across the probe's writes, falsified by any non-unique scheme incl. the same-second case) rather than the bump mechanism.
